steps.title: Use the relaxation measure
steps.content: The passage says reduced heart rate is a measure of relaxation. To support Moreno and colleagues' claim, the finding must show that infants' heart rates dropped during the lullaby.

steps.title: Rule out indirect evidence
steps.content: Choice A points to irregular heart rates, not relaxation. Choice C reports parents' preferences, not infants' physiological responses. Choice D describes the singers and does not show any calming effect.

steps.title: Match the physiological result
steps.content: The remaining finding directly shows substantially lower infant heart rates during the lullaby than during the non-lullaby. The answer is B.
